title: Valley of Flowers & Hemkund Sahib
summary: A striking dual alpine landscape: an emerald valley carpeted in hundreds of monsoon wildflower species, paired with a steep, punishing ascent to a sacred high-altitude glacial lake wrapped in mist.
story: Stepping into the Valley of Flowers feels like crossing the threshold into a living canvas. Hemmed in by massive, misty mountain walls, the valley floor transforms completely under monsoon showers, shifting its color palette weekly as rare endemic blooms compete for space.

The next dawn completely changes the tone. The trek up to Hemkund Sahib is a relentless, gravity-defying climb. Pushing through thin air at 4,632 meters, the rewards hit all at once: a completely silent crystal tarn, floating ice sheets, and the quiet devotion echoing from the world's highest Gurudwara.

title: Hampta Pass
summary: A dramatic crossover: lush green Kullu on one side, the stark cold desert of Spiti on the other, split by a single high pass.
story: Few treks change scenery as fast as Hampta. You start in pine forest and river meadows on the Kullu side and crest the pass into the moonscape of Spiti within a day.
The pass day is long and the descent is steep, but standing on the col with two completely different worlds on either side is hard to beat.

gallery.caption: River meadows on the Kullu side
The pass at first light
Crossing into Spiti
Chandratal blue

title: Kedarkantha
summary: A winter classic. Snow-loaded pines, a frozen summit climb at dawn, and a 360-degree ring of Himalayan peaks at the top.
story: Kedarkantha is the trek that hooks people on winter mountaineering. Snow from the first day, a magical campsite in the pines, and a summit dawn with peaks in every direction.
Simple, accessible, and unforgettable - the perfect introduction to snow.
